text: Last class I saw my progress and felt proud of how far I have come.
Today I want to cross a big city alone, by subway and bus.

You should buy a ticket before you get on.
You could take the bus or the subway.
The platform might be crowded at rush hour.
You should not miss your stop.
Excuse me, I want to go to the market. Which train should I take?
You should take the green line first. Get on here and ride four stops to Central Square.
And then? Do I stay on the same train, or do I transfer?
You transfer to the red line at Central Square. The market is two stops away. You might want to avoid rush hour, though.
Thank you so much. So I get off at Market Station. Could I check the schedule somewhere?
There is a screen on the platform. The trains run every five minutes, so you should not wait long. Have a good trip!
Which train should I take to the market?
Where can I buy a ticket?
Do I need to transfer to another line?
Which stop should I get off at?
How often do the trains run?

Good afternoon, passengers, and welcome aboard the metro. This is a service announcement. The next station is Central Square, where you can transfer to the blue line for the museum district and the airport express. Please mind the gap between the train and the platform when you get off. If you are traveling during rush hour, the trains might be crowded, so we suggest you hold the handrail and keep your bag close to you. Passengers with a day pass should remember that it is valid until midnight on all lines. If you miss your stop, do not worry. You could simply stay on the train and get off at the next station, then take the train going the other way. For information about routes and schedules, please ask a member of our staff or check the map on the wall. We wish you a pleasant journey. Thank you for choosing the city metro.

Hi Tuca, it is your friend from the hotel. I got your message about meeting me downtown tomorrow. Listen, taking the subway is much faster than a taxi, so here is what I suggest. From your hotel, you should walk to the station on the corner and buy a ticket at the ticket machine. It only takes coins or a card, and a single fare is very cheap. Take the green line and ride four stops to the central station. There, you might need to transfer to the red line. Just follow the signs, it is easy. The trains run every five minutes, so you could leave at any time, but try to avoid rush hour because the platform gets very crowded. When you get off at the market station, send me a message and I will meet you at the exit. See you tomorrow. Have a safe trip.

First, Tuca walked to the station on the corner. Then she bought a ticket at the machine and checked the fare. After that, she found the right platform and waited for her train. Next, she got on the green line and rode four stops to the center. Finally, she got off at the market station and met her friend at the exit.
